_doc: Template for a feature_spec.json record. Each accepted feature in the catalog gets one of these. Fill in placeholders; remove _doc/_note keys before committing.
_principle: ONE TECHNIQUE AXIS PER TEMPLATE, N INVOLVED FUZZERS STRATIFIED BY AXIS PRESENCE. Each template names exactly ONE technique axis (I2S or value_profile). Predictions and verification cover ALL involved fuzzers — partitioned into has_axis (predicted to win) and lacks_axis (predicted to lose). The anchor pair (pair.primary.A, .B) names the canonical highest-prob_div pair for the template's identity but does NOT cap the prediction set.
_path_b_change: 2026-05-06: relaxed from 'two fuzzers per template' (Path A) to 'one axis, N fuzzers' (Path B). Path A schema preserved at notes/archive/feature_spec.template.path-a.json.
id: <feature_id, snake_case, matches the template directory name>
_note_kind: kind is always 'local' under the current methodology — every feature is parameterizable by a program-side knob.

primary.A: <anchor winner — highest-prob_div pair's winner from the per-branch evidence>

involved_fuzzers: <all N fuzzers involved in decisive pairs at the source branches>
has_axis: <subset of involved_fuzzers that HAVE the axis technique — predicted winners>
lacks_axis: <subset of involved_fuzzers that LACK the axis technique — predicted losers>
pair._note_axis_partition: axis_differ partitions the canonical 4 fuzzers as follows: I2S → has={cmplog, value_profile_cmplog}, lacks={naive, value_profile}. value_profile → has={value_profile, value_profile_cmplog}, lacks={naive, cmplog}. The template's involved_fuzzers is a subset (>=2) of the canonical 4; has_axis and lacks_axis intersect involved_fuzzers with the canonical partition.

hypothesis.mechanism: <2-4 sentences explaining WHY the axis helps. Reference fuzzer-mechanism specifics (I2S substitution, CMP_MAP gradient, scheduler, mutator family) and connect them to the program-side parameter. The mechanism should explain ALL has_axis fuzzers' advantage uniformly — they share the technique, hence the same predicted curve shape.>
hypothesis.program_feature_parameter: <NAME (compile-time -D flag or runtime knob) — short description of what it controls>
hypothesis.predicted_direction: <has_axis fuzzers outperform lacks_axis fuzzers; ratio grows with PARAMETER>

predicted_endpoints.<PARAMETER>=<low>: <expected behavior at low end — describe per-cluster (has_axis cluster vs lacks_axis cluster) AND any within-cluster nuance>

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.<fuzzer>: <curve shape per involved fuzzer — e.g. 'flat-high (resolves at every dose)' or 'monotone-decreasing (drops as PARAMETER grows)' >

hypothesis._note_within_cluster_variance: Within has_axis (or lacks_axis), individual fuzzer curves may differ in offset/slope (e.g., vpc has both I2S and VP, so its absolute crash count may exceed cmp's at high dose). The PREDICTION is qualitative stratification: every has_axis fuzzer eventually beats every lacks_axis fuzzer at high PARAMETER. Quantitative within-cluster differences are observational.
hypothesis.tradeoff_observation: <OPTIONAL one paragraph: if a different axis (the OTHER technique) inverts the direction, or if cluster-internal effects matter (e.g., synergy where vpc > cmp under value_profile axis because vpc has VP fallback), describe it. Cross-axis findings warrant their OWN templates.>

verification.dockerfile: <path to Dockerfile or 'one per scan value, see deployed_targets'>
verification.trials_per_point: <int, recommend >=5 for clean signal, >=10 for bimodal cases>
verification.verdict: <one of: pending | reproduced | reproduced_in_median | partially_reproduced | refuted | inconclusive | refuted_due_to_<diagnosis>>
verification._note_results: Record every involved fuzzer's per-trial counts. The verdict is judged on whether stratification holds across involved fuzzers (every has_axis fuzzer >= every lacks_axis fuzzer at the relevant scan values).
verification._note_median: For bimodal fuzzers (naive especially), median is more honest than mean. Include both if useful.

summary.stratification_holds: <true | false | partial — does the has_axis cluster cleanly beat the lacks_axis cluster at the predicted endpoints?>

monotone_curves.<fuzzer>: <curve shape per involved fuzzer — e.g. 'exponential decay 993 -> 109 -> 0 -> 0'>

notes: <methodology lesson, harness caveat, surprising observation, or pointer to a separate template that covers a related (different-axis) finding>
